note: M9 fixture (docs/novakai/plans/m9-design.md) — ONE add change, m9-probe: a tiny pure function colocated in src/core/state/state.ts / state.novakai.mmd. Exists ONLY to drive tools/novakai/contract/loop-e2e.test.mjs's real FAIL->PASS verdict flip inside a throwaway sandbox worktree (git worktree add --detach <tmp> HEAD) — the real repo never gains this symbol; public/plan.json stays F5's untouched substrate. verdicts.m9-probe=accept exercises the --accepted-only human-decision path (approve-export). newNode.kind is set explicitly (function) so plan-cert and status.mjs cannot disagree about the node's kind. target.ref is the POST-BUNDLE namespaced id (`state__m9Probe`, matching every other real fixture's convention, e.g. unfold__ufVerbAllowed in m5-a-verbs.plan.json) because state.novakai.mmd is a non-global fragment (`%% root state`) and novakai-bundle.mjs prefixes every private id `<container>__<id>` at merge time; the bare local id inside the fragment stays `m9Probe` (matching its siblings createState/frameTransform/etc.), so the in-test implement step (13) feeds scaffold a bare-ref shim copy of this plan for the fragment write, then the real resync (step 14) is what produces the `state__m9Probe` id this plan tracks.

intent.problem: No existing fixture can drive a real FAIL->PASS verdict flip end to end: public/plan.json's only acceptance-bearing change (frame-transform) is already built, and every other unbuilt change in it is a real product feature that shouldn't be scaffolded just to exercise a test.
intent.approach: A disposable pure function, implemented ONLY inside the M9 sandbox worktree at test time (never in the real src tree), colocated with the existing state.ts/state.novakai.mmd pair so the fragment already carries the %% src convention the probe needs.
intent.rationale: M9 (docs/novakai/plans/m9-design.md) proves understand -> plan -> review -> approve -> implement -> verify end to end, with a real code-driven red->green flip that no mock can fake.

fm.name: m9Probe
fm.description: M9 loop probe: doubles a number. Throwaway — proves the verdict pipeline's FAIL->PASS flip; never lands in the real src tree.
